reason: The note attributes dominance to explanatory breadth ('explains too much of the disease architecture') rather than inertia or funding lock-in.

assertion.text: The amyloid cascade hypothesis persists as the dominant frame because no competing hypothesis simultaneously accounts for autosomal-dominant familial AD genetics (APP, PSEN1, PSEN2), Down syndrome AD risk, and amyloid-PET staging — not merely because of sociological inertia. Predictions: If a competing framework (e.g., tau-primary or endolysosomal dysfunction) were shown to account for APP/PSEN mutations and trisomy-21 risk, citation share and trial allocation for amyloid-targeting programs would decline within 3–5 years.; A survey of AD review articles 2020–2026 will show amyloid retains default-frame status in proportion to the number of architectural features it uniquely explains, not in proportion to trial success rate.

evidence_spans.text: The note attributes dominance to explanatory breadth ('explains too much of the disease architecture') rather than inertia or funding lock-in.

provenance.title: Amyloid cascade hypothesis remains dominant despite clinical failures.md
